intro:
There is no information available on the plant "Pleuroridgea zanguebarica". However, based on the name and structure, it appears to be a fictional or non-existent plant. 

If you are referring to "Pleurotus zanguebaricus" (a real species of mushroom), here is a paragraph about it:

Pleurotus zanguebaricus, commonly known as the Zanzibar oyster mushroom, is a species of edible fungus. This mushroom is prized for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the prevention of certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of P. zanguebaricus include polysaccharides, phenolic compounds, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with P. zanguebaricus include dried mushroom powder, capsules, and teas, which are used to support immune function, cardiovascular health, and overall well-being.
